Title: Complete biosynthesis of QS-21 in engineered yeast

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction; used to improve ER co-localization of P450s
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression; confocal co-localization with oxidases; LC–MS quantification of QA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SvMSBP1 acted as a scaffold candidate that co-localized with C28 and C23 oxidases on the ER membrane. 2. Its expression increased QA production about fourfold. 3. It provides an effective strategy to improve plant P450 performance in yeast.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as a pathway-organizing scaffold rather than an enzyme or transcriptional regulator.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances QA accumulation by organizing P450 oxidation modules on the ER membrane.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising universal scaffold component to improve heterologous P450 pathways in microbial engineering.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: chromosomal copy present in engineered yeast background
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Requirement inferred from successful detection of 2MB-CoA in strain containing chromosomal npgA and LovF-TE plus QsCCL
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A chromosomal npgA copy was included to support phosphopantetheinylation of the engineered PKS module. 2. In this background, LovF-TE and QsCCL produced detectable 2MB-CoA. 3. This support function enabled complete sugar-only QS-21 biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accessory post-translational activation factor, not a catalytic step in the QS-21 skeleton itself.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables engineered PKS activity for endogenous 2MB precursor supply.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory gene when deploying fungal PKSs in yeast.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to β-amyrin.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent vaccine adjuvant that stimulates/augments immune responses.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose-inducible production phase after growth in glucose-containing YPD
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast; GC–MS metabolite profiling; comparison of BAS homologues
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful entry enzyme for microbial reconstruction of QS-21 and related triterpene saponins; suitable with enhanced mevalonate flux and ERG20/ERG1 upregulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among BAS homologues tested, SvBAS gave the highest β-amyrin titre in yeast. 2. SvBAS efficiently consumed squalene and produced the triterpene precursor for downstream QS-21 biosynthesis. 3. Combined with host mevalonate-pathway engineering, it enabled high β-amyrin production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Other BAS homologues from Artemisia annua, Arabidopsis thaliana and Glycyrrhiza glabra were tested but were not selected as the core production enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes high β-amyrin accumulation, enabling efficient entry into the QS-21 pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Bottleneck among the three P450 oxidation modules; overexpression gave the largest increase in QA and additional CPR further improved titre
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: oleanolic acid via sequential C28 oxidation intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Carries out three sequential oxidations at C28 of β-amyrin to form the carboxylic acid, producing oleanolic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent vaccine adjuvant that stimulates/augments immune responses.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose-inducible production; co-expression with CPR and MSBP improved oxidation efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sequential pathway reconstruction in yeast; LC–MS detection of oleanolic acid; gene dosage optimization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key oxidation enzyme to include in engineered QS-21 or quillaic-acid platforms; should be paired with adequate CPR/redox support and scaffold-assisted membrane co-localization.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP716A224 with CPR was sufficient for the three-step C28 oxidation of β-amyrin to oleanolic acid. 2. Overexpression of CYP716A224 caused the strongest increase in QA production among the P450s tested. 3. A second CPR copy further boosted QA, indicating this module is a major bottleneck.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as donor of the N-terminal TMD fused to CYP716A297 for proper ER localization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flux from β-amyrin into oxidized triterpene intermediates and is a major determinant of QA accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: P450 redox support for β-amyrin oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: electrons/redox support for P450 C23 oxidation system
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts as a cytochrome b5 redox partner required to support C23 oxidation leading to aldehyde formation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction; functions with P450 oxidation module
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis in reconstructed yeast pathway; LC–MS detection of gypsogenin formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important auxiliary component for efficient plant P450 oxidation modules in heterologous hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Qsb5 was required for efficient C23 oxidation to the aldehyde-containing product gypsogenin. 2. CPR alone was insufficient for this oxidation state transition. 3. Inclusion of cytochrome b5 improved functional reconstitution of the Quillaja oxidation pathway in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified here as pathway-supporting redox protein because it is a core experimental subject in the reconstructed biosynthetic module.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables C23 oxidation needed for formation of downstream QA.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; overexpression improved QA about twofold
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: oleanolic-acid-type intermediate(s) in β-amyrin oxidation sequence
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: gypsogenin via C23 oxidation to aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes the C23 position to generate the aldehyde functionality of gypsogenin.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Requires co-expression with Qsb5 and CPR for effective oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstruction in yeast; LC–MS detection of gypsogenin and QA; copy-number optimization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary oxidation module for biosynthetic access to QA and downstream saponin adjuvants.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The C23 oxidase was necessary to form the aldehyde-containing intermediate gypsogenin. 2. Efficient activity depended on inclusion of Quillaja cytochrome b5. 3. Additional gene copy increased QA titre, indicating suboptimal baseline activity in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene symbol not explicitly given in the provided text but corresponds to the Quillaja C23 oxidase characterized previously for the QS-21 pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of gypsogenin and supports downstream QA production.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; overexpression improved QA about twofold
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: gypsogenin or related oxidized β-amyrin intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates C16 to convert gypsogenin-type substrate to quillaic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosolic when codon optimized alone; ER membrane when fused with CYP716A224 TMD
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Functional only after N-terminal TMD fusion to restore ER membrane localization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization by confocal microscopy; fusion engineering; LC–MS detection of QA formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Illustrates importance of membrane targeting when expressing plant P450s in microbes; TMD engineering can rescue activity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Yeast codon-optimized CYP716A297 was mislocalized to the cytosol and inactive. 2. Fusion of the CYP716A224 transmembrane domain redirected the enzyme to the ER membrane. 3. Correct localization enabled conversion of gypsogenin to quillaic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The active form used in pathway construction was the TMD_C28–C16 fusion protein.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: After ER retargeting, enables QA production by catalyzing the final C16 oxidation step.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Redox support became limiting; second copy increased QA in C28-overexpressing strain
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers electrons from NADPH to cytochrome P450 enzymes in the QS-21 oxidation pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction; dosage influenced oxidation flux
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with P450s in yeast; copy-number increase; LC–MS quantification of QA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential redox partner for reconstructing plant P450 cascades in microbial hosts; co-optimization with P450 dosage is important.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtATR1 supported the Quillaja P450 oxidation steps in yeast. 2. Additional ATR1 copy raised QA titre in the C28-oxidase overexpression background. 3. Redox-partner supply is therefore a significant engineering lever for QS-21 aglycone production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a catalyst of the carbon skeleton transformation itself, it is essential to pathway function and was experimentally optimized.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves QA formation by enhancing P450 electron transfer.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry step to glycosylated QS-21 branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucuronic acid to the C3 hydroxyl of quillaic acid to form QA-C3-GlcA.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like M family glycosyltransferase with multiple transmembrane domains
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Requires heterologous UDP-GlcA supply via AtUGD1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with AtUGD1; LC–MS detection of QA-C3-GlcA; comparison with CSLM1
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred enzyme for microbial glucuronidation of QA; critical first glycosylation in QS-21 assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLM2 glucuronidated QA in yeast to produce QA-C3-GlcA. 2. It also acted on less oxidized intermediates but showed highest activity toward QA. 3. Because of higher useful activity and pathway compatibility, CSLM2 was chosen for further engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CSLM1 also functioned, but CSLM2 was selected as the main core enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates the glycosylation branch leading from QA toward QS-21.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nucleotide sugar supply for QS-21 biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for overcoming UDP-Xyl feedback inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes UDP-glucose to UDP-glucuronic acid; A101L variant reduces feedback inhibition by UDP-xylose.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UDP-Xyl feedback inhibition was an important metabolic constraint
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutational engineering (A101L); co-expression with AtUXS3 and xylosyltransferase; LC–MS detection of xylosylated product 9
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates value of enzyme variants resistant to allosteric inhibition for balancing nucleotide-sugar pools in microbial production systems.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wild-type AtUGD1 was inhibited in the presence of UDP-Xyl pathway flux, blocking downstream xylosylation. 2. The A101L variant alleviated feedback inhibition. 3. This enabled production of the C3-xylosylated intermediate 9.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unmodified AtUGD1 was initially used for glucuronidation with CSLMs; the A101L mutation was introduced later for better pathway balance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases UDP-GlcA availability under UDP-Xyl-producing conditions and enables downstream glycosylation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose to QA-C3-GlcA through a 1,2-glycosidic linkage to yield the second C3 sugar.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction; benefits from endogenous UDP-Gal availability
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stepwise pathway reconstruction; LC–MS detection and quantification of product 8
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for extending glucuronidated triterpene scaffolds and for pulling flux through early glycosylation steps.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73CU3 efficiently galactosylated QA-C3-GlcA to produce 8. 2. Its expression increased overall conversion of QA by consuming intermediate 7. 3. This pull effect substantially improved flux through the early glycosylation pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cytosolic localization is consistent with proposed compartment transition after C3 glucuronidation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of 7 to 8 and increases pathway flux toward fully glycosylated QS-21 intermediates.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nucleotide sugar supply for QS-21 biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: UDP-Xyl supply became limiting for both C3 and C28 xylosylation; additional copy relieved bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates UDP-glucuronic acid to produce UDP-xylose.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flux affected by UGD feedback inhibition and xylosyltransferase demand
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with C3XylT/C28XylT3; copy-number increase; LC–MS analysis of intermediate accumulation and product formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical for balancing nucleotide-sugar pools where multiple xylosyltransferases compete for UDP-Xyl.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtUXS3 was required to supply UDP-Xyl for C3 and C28 xylosylation. 2. Initial UDP-Xyl synthesis also triggered UGD inhibition, necessitating UGD engineering. 3. An additional AtUXS copy relieved accumulation of intermediates and enabled 12 as the major product.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as AtUXS in some sections; same functional role.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Boosts UDP-Xyl supply and improves completion of xylosylation steps in the QS-21 pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose to complete the branched C3 trisaccharide on the quillaic-acid scaffold.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Requires sufficient UDP-Xyl and feedback-relieved AtUGD1 variant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with AtUXS3 and AtUGD1(A101L); LC–MS detection of 9
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core enzyme for assembling the C3 branched glycan in engineered QS-21 analog production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73CX1 catalysed the third C3 glycosylation step to produce 9. 2. Its activity was only realized after engineering nucleotide-sugar homeostasis. 3. It establishes the complete C3 trisaccharide required for downstream C28 decoration and acylation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper later notes UGT73CX2 can replace it to generate a methylated/rhamnosylated derivative branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes the native C3 trisaccharide assembly toward QS-21.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nucleotide sugar supply for QS-21 biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts UDP-glucose to UDP-4-dehydro-6-deoxy-D-glucose in the UDP-D-fucose biosynthetic route.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with FucSyn and UGT74BX1; LC–MS detection of fucosylated product 10
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Needed to establish non-native UDP-D-Fuc supply in microbial hosts for triterpene glycoengineering.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SvUG46DH supplied the deoxy-sugar intermediate needed for UDP-D-Fuc formation. 2. Together with FucSyn and C28FucT it enabled formation of the C28-fucosylated intermediate 10. 3. It expanded yeast nucleotide-sugar capability beyond native metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species differs from most Quillaja glycosyltransferases, illustrating cross-species combinatorial pathway design.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables de novo synthesis of the C28 fucosyl donor required for downstream QS-21 assembly.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nucleotide sugar supply for QS-21 biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the dehydro-deoxy UDP-sugar intermediate to produce UDP-D-fucose for C28 glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with SvUG46DH and UGT74BX1; LC–MS detection of product 10
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for installing rare deoxy sugars in engineered saponins.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FucSyn completed UDP-D-fucose biosynthesis in yeast. 2. Along with SvUG46DH and C28FucT, it enabled production of the C28-fucosylated intermediate. 3. This step opened the C28 oligosaccharide branch of QS-21 assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides UDP-D-fucose needed for C28 glycosylation.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry step to C28 oligosaccharide assembly
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers D-fucose to the C28 carboxyl group by ester linkage to initiate the linear C28 sugar chain.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Requires UDP-D-Fuc biosynthetic enzymes; pathway flux improved by downstream rhamnosylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with UDP-D-Fuc supply genes; LC–MS detection of 10 and flux-pulling by downstream enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Crucial for constructing the pharmacologically important C28 oligosaccharide branch of QS-21 and derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74BX1 initiated C28 sugar-chain assembly by esterifying D-fucose to the C28 carboxyl group. 2. Its product accumulated more effectively when downstream C28RhaT was present. 3. This step committed pathway intermediates to C28 glycan elaboration.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residual substrate remained when UGT74BX1 acted without downstream pull, indicating flux coupling across successive glycosylations.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates C28 tetrasaccharide assembly required for mature QS-21.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nucleotide sugar supply for QS-21 biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes UDP-L-rhamnose used for the second sugar of the C28 tetrasaccharide.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with UGT91AR1 and upstream C28 module; LC–MS monitoring of complete conversion to 11
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports production of rhamnosylated triterpene saponins in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtRHM2 supplied UDP-Rha for the second C28 glycosylation step. 2. Together with C28RhaT it fully converted intermediate 10 to 11. 3. This downstream pull increased effective pathway flux through the C28FucT step.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides UDP-Rha, enabling efficient formation of the C28 disaccharide intermediate.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose to the C28-fucosylated intermediate to extend the linear tetrasaccharide chain.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Requires UDP-Rha from AtRHM2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with AtRHM2; LC–MS showing full conversion of 10 to 11
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to pull flux through C28 glycan assembly in engineered saponin pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT91AR1 efficiently converted 10 to 11. 2. This downstream reaction pulled the equilibrium of the preceding C28 fucosylation step. 3. It improved pathway flux into longer C28 oligosaccharide intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes complete conversion of 10 to 11 and enhances C28 branch flux.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Its substrate demand exposed UDP-Xyl limitation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose as the third sugar in the C28 linear tetrasaccharide chain.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UDP-Xyl became limiting because both C3 and C28 xylosyltransferases competed for donor supply
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression in pathway strains; LC–MS analysis of intermediate accumulation; rescue by extra AtUXS copy
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights need for donor-sugar balancing when multiple glycosyltransferases compete for the same precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT91AQ1 added the third sugar on the C28 chain to produce 12. 2. Accumulation of 8 and 11 after its expression revealed UDP-Xyl limitation. 3. Increasing AtUXS expression relieved this bottleneck and made 12 the major product.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Extends the C28 tetrasaccharide but creates strong demand for UDP-Xyl.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C28 terminal sugar branch toward QS-21-Xyl
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal-step bottleneck due to misfolding in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds terminal xylose to the C28 sugar chain to form the Xyl-ended hepta-glycosylated intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed under galactose-inducible promoters; better fresh-medium reinduction improved cytosolic expression
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol with time-dependent aggregation adjacent to vacuole
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Protein stability improved by fresh YP galactose feeding and renewed induction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GFP localization; LC–MS detection of trace 13 and improved product after culture scheme adjustment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A major protein-engineering target for improving QS-21-Xyl titres in yeast because terminal xylosylation remained inefficient.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73CY3 catalysed the terminal C28 xylosylation toward the QS-21-Xyl branch. 2. In yeast it tended to misfold and aggregate, causing only trace production initially. 3. Reinduction in fresh galactose medium improved cytosolic expression and supported downstream acylation and final QS-21-Xyl biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shares 94.89% sequence identity with UGT73CY2 and shows similar instability in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces the hepta-glycosylated precursor 13 for QS-21-Xyl, but low protein stability limits flux.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C28 terminal sugar branch toward QS-21-Api
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal-step bottleneck due to misfolding in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds terminal apiose to the C28 sugar chain to form the Api-ended hepta-glycosylated intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol with time-dependent aggregation adjacent to vacuole
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Protein instability in yeast limited product formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GFP localization; LC–MS detection of trace 14 and downstream successful QS-21-Api assembly in engineered strains
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering stability/expression of this enzyme should improve QS-21-Api production in microbial systems.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73CY2 directed the terminal C28 apiose branch to form 14. 2. Like UGT73CY3, it was prone to misfolding and aggregation in yeast. 3. Despite low efficiency, the generated 14 supported final biosynthesis of QS-21-Api.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highly homologous to UGT73CY3, likely explaining similar heterologous-expression behavior.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces the hepta-glycosylated precursor 14 for QS-21-Api, but instability limits titre.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-keto acyl intermediate in C9-CoA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses decarboxylative Claisen condensation reactions contributing to C9 acyl-chain formation from 2MB-CoA and malonyl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent vaccine adjuvant; acyl chain is crucial to immunoactivity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed during galactose induction; tested with 2MB acid supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Required exogenous 2MB acid supplementation or engineered endogenous 2MB supply
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression with PKS5/KR1/KR2 and ACT2 in yeast; indirect functional validation by LC–MS detection of mono-acylated product 19
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Part of the minimal cassette for installing the immunologically important dimeric acyl moiety onto QS-21 intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PKS4 was part of the reconstructed acyl biosynthetic cassette for C9-CoA synthesis. 2. Direct C9-CoA detection was difficult, but pathway function was demonstrated by downstream acylated saponin production. 3. It enabled formation of the acyl unit critical for potent QS-21 activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts together with PKS5 and stand-alone ketoreductases; individual contribution was not dissected separately.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to synthesis of the C9 acyl donor required for final QS-21 acylation.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-keto acyl intermediate in C9-CoA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses decarboxylative Claisen condensation reactions contributing to C9 acyl-chain formation from 2MB-CoA and malonyl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent vaccine adjuvant; acyl chain is crucial to immunoactivity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed during galactose induction; tested with 2MB acid supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Required exogenous 2MB acid supplementation or engineered endogenous 2MB supply
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression with PKS4/KR1/KR2 and ACT2 in yeast; indirect functional validation by LC–MS detection of mono-acylated product 19
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Part of acyl donor biosynthesis cassette for constructing active QS-21 analogues.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PKS5 cooperated with PKS4 and ketoreductases in C9-CoA biosynthesis. 2. Functional activity was inferred from successful acylation of glycosylated intermediates. 3. It is required for assembly of the biologically important dimeric acyl appendage.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Individual mechanistic partitioning between PKS4 and PKS5 was not resolved in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to production of the C9 acyl donor used in final QS-21 assembly.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces keto intermediates generated during C9-CoA biosynthesis to help form the 3,5-dihydroxy acyl moiety.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent vaccine adjuvant; acyl chain is crucial to immunoactivity.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dependent on 2MB donor availability
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cassette expression with PKS4/PKS5/KR2 and indirect validation through acylated product detection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required component for generating the correctly reduced acyl chain of QS-21 analogues.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KR1 was part of the acyl cassette reconstructing the C9 donor pathway. 2. It contributes to formation of the dihydroxy pattern in the acyl chain. 3. Functional cassette operation was demonstrated by downstream mono- and di-acylated saponin products.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct standalone assay reported.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports production of the correct reduced C9 acyl donor for QS-21 acylation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces keto intermediates generated during C9-CoA biosynthesis to help form the 3,5-dihydroxy acyl moiety.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent vaccine adjuvant; acyl chain is crucial to immunoactivity.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dependent on 2MB donor availability
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cassette expression with PKS4/PKS5/KR1 and indirect validation through acylated product detection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required for reconstituting the complete acyl donor module in microbial QS-21 production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KR2 was included with PKS4/PKS5/KR1 to build the C9 acyl biosynthetic cassette. 2. The cassette functioned in vivo as shown by production of acylated QS-21 intermediates. 3. KR2 helps establish the final dihydroxy acyl-chain structure.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct standalone assay reported.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports biosynthesis of the reduced C9 acyl donor used for QS-21 acylation.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important enabling step for non-native 2MB-CoA formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates exogenous or endogenously released 2-methylbutyric acid to 2-methylbutyryl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent vaccine adjuvant; acyl chain is crucial to immunoactivity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in yeast; tested with 2MB acid feeding and with LovF-TE module
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 2MB acid supplementation (50–500 mg l−1) markedly affected acylation; later paired with engineered LovF-TE for sugar-only production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LC–MS detection of 2MB-CoA; acylated-product formation after cassette expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key enzyme for supplying unusual starter CoA thioesters in microbial natural-product engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. QsCCL1 converted supplemented 2MB acid into intracellular 2MB-CoA. 2. This enabled acyl-unit biosynthesis and subsequent mono- and di-acylation of QS-21 intermediates. 3. QsCCL1 also functioned with engineered LovF-TE to support sugar-only de novo QS-21 biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct 2MB-CoA detection was also achieved when paired with engineered LovF-TE and npgA background.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides 2MB-CoA starter unit required for building the C9 acyl donor.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hexa- or hepta-glycosylated intermediates (12 or 13) and C9-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers one C9 acyl unit to the fucose-linked glycosylated triterpene intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent vaccine adjuvant; acyl chain is crucial to immunoactivity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed during galactose induction; improved by higher 2MB acid feeding
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Acylation conversion improved strongly with higher 2MB acid supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integration into 12-producing strain; LC–MS detection of mono-acylated products; co-elution with tobacco extract standard
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential for installing the first acyl unit and for generating structurally diversified QS-21 analogues.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACT2 acylated glycosylated intermediates in vivo to generate mono-acylated products. 2. It could act on both 13 and the hexa-glycosylated intermediate 12. 3. Higher 2MB precursor feeding improved ACT2-mediated conversion.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally permissive substrate scope aided pathway progression even before complete terminal xylosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds the first C9 acyl moiety required for downstream di-acylation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a second C9 acyl unit to form the dimeric C18 acyl appendage on QS-21 intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent vaccine adjuvant; acyl chain is crucial to immunoactivity.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dependent on availability of 2MB-derived C9-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression in engineered strains; LC–MS detection of di-acylated products 17 and 20
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required to generate the full dimeric acyl structure linked to high adjuvant potency.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACT3 fully acylated mono-acylated intermediates to give di-acylated products. 2. It acted on both xylosylated and non-terminally xylosylated mono-acylated substrates. 3. This established the complete dimeric acyl moiety before terminal Araf addition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Results imply the acyl donor supply was sufficient for two sequential acyl transfers once precursor pathway was installed.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes formation of the dimeric C18 acyl appendage needed before final arabinofuranosylation.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nucleotide sugar supply for QS-21 biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctional epimerase that interconverts UDP-Glc/UDP-Gal and UDP-Xyl/UDP-Arap, supplying UDP-Arap for UDP-Araf biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Chosen as cytosolic homologue because yeast lacks plant-like Golgi nucleotide sugar transport system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with AtRGP1 and UGT73CZ2; LC–MS/QTOF detection of QS-21-Xyl peak
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing cytosolic arabinofuranose donor pathways in non-plant hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtUGE1 supplied UDP-Arap in the cytosol for final arabinofuranose donor synthesis. 2. It bypassed the need for Golgi-localized plant arabinose metabolism. 3. Combined with AtRGP1 and ArafT, it enabled final QS-21 production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bifunctional activity on both hexose and pentose UDP-sugars was explicitly noted.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies UDP-Arap precursor for final Araf addition and complete QS-21 biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nucleotide sugar supply for QS-21 biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts UDP-arabinopyranose to UDP-arabinofuranose for the final glycosylation step.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Chosen as cytosolic enzyme for compatibility with yeast host
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with AtUGE1 and UGT73CZ2; LC–MS/QTOF detection of final product co-eluting with QS-21 standard
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical for installing terminal arabinofuranose in microbial glycoengineering of plant natural products.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtRGP1 generated UDP-Araf in vivo in yeast. 2. This donor enabled the final arabinofuranose transfer that completed QS-21 biosynthesis. 3. Its cytosolic implementation bypassed the absent plant Golgi trafficking system in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables synthesis of the terminal Araf donor required for complete QS-21 formation.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant of completed QS-21 scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: QS-21-Xyl (and analogous Api form in separate branch)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers terminal α-L-arabinofuranose to the acyl chain moiety, completing QS-21 biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Requires AtUGE1/AtRGP1-mediated UDP-Araf supply; product analysis performed with and without 2MB supplementation depending strain background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with UDP-Araf biosynthetic genes; LC–QTOF co-elution with authentic standard; isotopic fingerprinting; HRMS2; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical terminal enzyme for complete microbial QS-21 production and for generating analogues through donor/promiscuity engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73CZ2 catalysed the final arabinofuranosylation step completing QS-21 biosynthesis in yeast. 2. The resulting product co-eluted with the QS-21 standard and was validated by isotopic fingerprint, HRMS2 and NMR. 3. Enzyme promiscuity likely also generated a minor Xyl-capped side product.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes a preceding peak likely reflects promiscuity of the Araf transferase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly forms QS-21 in engineered yeast.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: engineered type I polyketide synthase releasing 2-methylbutyrate
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Aspergillus terreus (LovF) and Saccharopolyspora erythraea-derived erythromycin PKS TE
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Endogenous 2MB acid generation from simple sugars
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: free 2-methylbutyric acid (then converted to 2MB-CoA by QsCCL1)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Engineered truncated LovF fused to an erythromycin PKS thioesterase synthesizes methylbutyryl-S-ACP and hydrolyses it to release free 2-methylbutyric acid.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type I fungal PKS fused to PKS thioesterase domain
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Enabled production without exogenous 2MB acid supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein engineering; LC–MS detection of 2MB-CoA when co-expressed with QsCCL1; final QS-21 production from simple sugars
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Powerful strategy to generate non-native short-chain acyl precursors intracellularly and eliminate precursor feeding in industrial QS-21 fermentation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Engineered LovF-TE released free 2MB acid from a PKS-bound intermediate in yeast. 2. Co-expression with QsCCL1 yielded detectable 2MB-CoA. 3. Integration of this cassette enabled complete QS-21 biosynthesis from simple sugars without exogenous 2MB supplementation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The source species are mixed because the construct combines fungal LovF and a heterologous erythromycin PKS thioesterase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables de novo 2MB precursor supply for fully sugar-fed QS-21 production.
